'''Tom Armstrong''' is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played primarily at {{rlp|ce}}, but also deputised on the {{rlp|wg}} and the [[Rugby league positions#The back row|back row]]. 

He played for amateur side [[Pilkington Recs]] before joining [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. Armstrong also played for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Career==
Armstrong's début game for Saints came against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the first round of [[Super League XIV]]. Standing in for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international [[Francis Meli]], he scored a try in a 26-14 win.<ref name="Saints 26-14 Warrington">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/88|title=Saints 26-14 Warrington|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-07-01|accessdate=2009-07-01}}</ref> He went on from this to play against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the next round,<ref name="Saints 23-6 Huddersfield">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/89|title=Saints 23-6 Huddersfield|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-07-01|accessdate=2009-07-01}}</ref> [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]<ref name="Saints 18-19 Hull KR">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/94|title=Saints 18-19 Hull KR|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-07-01|accessdate=2009-07-01}}</ref> and a try scoring performance in a 4-0 win over [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] (he was the only player to score in this game) <ref name="Saints 4-0 Celtic">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/94|title=Saints 4-0 Celtic|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-07-01|accessdate=2009-07-01}}</ref> After a period in the reserves, he came back to play in the win over [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] where he scored a try in a 44-24 victory.<ref name="Saints 44-24 Harlequins">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/109|title=Saints 44-24 Harlequins|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-07-25|accessdate=2009-07-27}}</ref>

It took Armstrong 13 rounds to break back into the first-team. At the [[Magic Weekend]], held at [[Murrayfield Stadium|Murrayfield]], he scored a try from left centre in a 54-0 win over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], in his first game of the season.<ref name="St Helens 54-0 Hull KR">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3274222|title=St Helens 54-0 Hull KR|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2010-05-02|accessdate=2010-05-02}}</ref>

Armstrong was dual registered with [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for 2011's [[Super League XVI]].<ref name="Youngster Armstrong links up with Leigh">{{cite web|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/saints/news/8632064.Youngster_Armstrong_links_up_with_Leigh/|title=Youngster Armstrong links up with Leigh|publisher=St Helens Star|date=2010-11-11|accessdate=2010-11-12}}</ref> Armstrong scored the winning try in Leigh's 20-16 win over [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the 2011 [[Northern Rail Cup]] Final.{{fact|date=January 2024}}

Armstrong was released from his contract with St. Helens and has decided to join [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] ahead of the 2012 season.{{fact|date=January 2024}}

Armstrong was forced to retire from the professional game in 2018 due to [[osteoarthritis]].<ref>{{cite web |title=The 3am moment with my baby boy that made me realise I had a drinking problem |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/news/greater-manchester-news/the-3am-moment-baby-boy-25644638 |website=Manchester Evening News |access-date=3 November 2024 |date=4 December 2022}}</ref> In 2024, he joined [[Fylde RFC]] as a player-coach.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tom Armstrong joins Fylde as S&C Coach/player for 2024-25 |url=https://www.fylderugby.co.uk/tom-armstrong-joins-fylde-as-sc-coach-player-for-2024-25/ |publisher=Fylde RFC |access-date=3 November 2024}}</ref>
